title: George Washington: Father of Our Country
description: This coloring page features George Washington in a historical setting with elements from colonial America. Students will learn about our first president while enjoying a creative activity.
instructions: Color the image of George Washington and the colonial elements. Use the suggested colors or choose your own. Take your time with details like Washington's uniform and the American flag.

content.image: George Washington standing tall in his Continental Army uniform with a tricorn hat. He is positioned near the Delaware River with a colonial flag, his horse, and Mount Vernon in the background. The scene includes a cherry tree and colonial soldiers.
learningFacts: George Washington was born on February 22, 1732
He became the first President of the United States in 1789
Washington led the Continental Army during the American Revolution
He lived at his plantation called Mount Vernon in Virginia
Washington served two terms as president and established many presidential traditions
